marketState: neutral — bearish near-term momentum offset by extreme short positioning and seasonal upside risk into fall
summary: Front-month NG sits almost exactly at fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923 fair, within a wide $2.42-$3.42 band), so there is no clean mispricing edge in either direction. Seasonality is weak-bearish but explicitly flagged Low reliability (59pp 10-90 spread), while above-5y-normal cooling demand (+9.8% 1-7D, +6.2% 8-16D) is a supportive counterweight. Storage is bearish near-term (+180 Bcf surplus vs 5y, a +22 Bcf bearish build surprise) and the daily/weekly technical trend is down (EMA50<200, negative MACD, RSI mid-30s), arguing for near-term softness. Longer out, managed-money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range — an historically stretched positioning extreme that raises short-covering/reversal risk, especially heading into September, the seasonally strongest month in the 16-year sample (+3.1% avg). The futures curve is a normal seasonal shape (slight front backwardation into Sep/Oct, then a large winter contango peaking near Jan 2027), so curve structure adds no unusual signal. Net: lean short-term bearish continuation, longer-term modest reversal-higher risk, both with limited conviction given the low seasonality reliability and no production data in this snapshot.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, consider entering half now / half on confirmation
longTerm.rationale: Managed-money net-short at the 8th percentile of its 5y range is a contrarian bullish extreme, demand is running 6-10% above 5y normal, and the calendar is heading into September, historically the strongest month (+3.1% avg); fair value also drifts up toward $3.01 by +21D. This is offset by a bearish storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y) and Low-reliability seasonality (probHigher 3-4wk only 38%), which caps conviction.
longTerm.invalidation: Managed-money shorts extend further without covering, storage surprises keep coming in bearish, and price breaks and holds below $2.70

shortTerm.rationale: Daily and weekly trends are bearish (EMA50<200, negative MACD, RSI ~37) and the latest storage build surprised bearish (+22 Bcf vs forecast), while front price already sits at fair value so there is no fundamental floor to lean on. Above-normal near-term cooling demand, a slightly bullish 15m/1h intraday tape, and a modest Aug/Sep front backwardation argue against chasing the short too hard, hence low conviction and a tight stop.
shortTerm.invalidation: Price reclaims and holds above $3.05, or 1h/4h EMA crosses turn bullish alongside a bullish storage surprise

Daily/weekly technicals in a bearish downtrend (EMA50<200, negative MACD)
Front price essentially at fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923), inside a wide $2.42-$3.42 band
risks: Seasonality reliability flagged Low (59pp 10-90 spread) — weak edge from the seasonal pattern
Extreme short COT positioning can persist or extend further before any reversal (not a timing signal)
